reforms.description: The Finnish government plans to implement a comprehensive nationwide reform to restructure municipalities and services, aiming to enhance administrative structures, productivity, and effectiveness.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving administrative efficiency and productivity in municipalities can lead to better service delivery and resource allocation, supporting overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced a comprehensive nationwide reform to restructure municipalities and services.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change aimed at improving the efficiency and effectiveness of local governance, which is crucial for addressing long-term fiscal challenges and enhancing public service delivery.
reforms.source_quote: The government has announced a 'comprehensive nationwide reform to restructure municipalities and services, building on economically robust municipalities, enabling improvements in administrative structures, productivity and the effectiveness of municipalities.'

reforms.description: The Finnish government is considering a shift towards a more generalized R&D subsidy system, potentially through tax credits, to improve flexibility and efficiency in supporting innovation, particularly for small start-ups. This includes moving from project-based R&D support to a more flexible R&D tax credit system, aiming to enhance efficiency and equity in funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The shift to a more flexible R&D tax credit system is expected to stimulate private sector innovation and improve the overall efficiency of R&D funding.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is considering these changes, indicating they have not yet been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the way R&D support is provided, moving towards a more efficient and equitable system.
reforms.source_quote: The government should consider whether a move towards a more generalized R&D subsidy system, e.g. through tax credits, would improve flexibility and efficiency.

reforms.description: The Finnish government aims to support entrepreneurship and innovation by improving the quality of start-ups and providing opportunities for promising firms to expand quickly. This includes reducing institutional barriers to entrepreneurship and enhancing access to private sector finance for start-ups through equity financing options like business angels and venture capital.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the startup ecosystem is expected to boost productivity and innovation by facilitating the growth of new firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has been providing direct support to start-ups and SMEs through various financial instruments.
reforms.importance_rationale: These initiatives represent significant but narrower changes aimed at improving the startup environment.
reforms.source_quote: Conditions for start-ups are broadly favourable in Finland... However, institutional barriers to entrepreneurship tend to be slightly higher than in the average OECD country.

reforms.description: The Finnish government plans to maintain strong support for basic R&D and education by distributing research grants based on academic performance, while also streamlining support for businesses and private sector R&D to focus on academic environments that reward quality.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Maintaining strong support for basic R&D is essential for enhancing the quality of research output, which contributes to long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has implemented plans to distribute research grants based on academic performance.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment in the allocation of public R&D funding.
reforms.source_quote: Current support for businesses and private sector R&D do not seem to be effective and should be scaled back further and streamlined together with business taxation.

reforms.description: The Finnish government introduced a new Innovation Strategy in 2008, which aims to redirect public R&D spending towards non-technological areas such as public services and private sector innovation, enhancing cooperation between public and private sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Redirecting R&D spending towards non-technological areas is expected to foster innovation in public services and enhance overall productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Innovation Strategy was introduced and has been in effect since 2008.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the approach to public R&D spending.
reforms.source_quote: The government introduced a new Innovation Strategy in 2008, which includes funnelling a larger share of public R&D spending towards non-technological areas such as the public sector and private services.

reforms.description: Finland has encouraged the separation of purchaser and provider functions in health care, allowing municipalities to adopt this model to improve efficiency and user choice.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving efficiency and user choice in health care is expected to enhance overall productivity in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: Municipalities have been adopting this model to improve health care efficiency.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment in the organization of health care services.
reforms.source_quote: In Finland, purchaser-provider separation has been encouraged by the central government and adopted by several municipalities in the 2000s.
